methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Rain AI is a Series A chip design company founded by Gordon Wilson, currently carrying a $500M post-money valuation after raising $146M. The valuation analysis draws on a frontier-semiconductors peer set comprising 3 publicly traded comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers, with particular focus on same-stage players Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), and Substrate ($1.0B).

Two methods triangulate the valuation. Stage-Progression applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier from the $500M Series A post-money, yielding $1.0B. Peer-Median across the three same-sector, same-stage private comparables produces $2.0B, with a range of $1.0B to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring is not applicable; while the Semiconductors sector trades at a 25.3x median P/S multiple, Rain AI's revenue is not disclosed, preventing revenue-based valuation.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $850M to $2.3B, with a median of $1.5B. This implies modest upside from current valuation under conservative assumptions, but substantial upside toward the peer median. Key uncertainties include the comparability of Etched's outlier $5.0B valuation, the absence of revenue or profitability metrics to anchor absolute value, and whether frontier-chip capital intensity justifies peer multiples. A Series B round would test whether Rain AI's progress supports the peer-median band.
